claims.statement: The stellar hydrostatic-equilibrium anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when inward gravity is balanced by outward pressure gradients inside the star.

constraints: hydrostatic support must be internal to the star
the balance is structural, not a planetary shape threshold
the same balance feeds later thermal and compositional stages

sources.citation: NASA Technical Reports Server, Stellar evolution: A survey with analytic.
sources.note: Hydrostatic equilibrium as one of the fundamental stellar-structure equations.

sources.citation: ESA Science & Technology, Stellar Processes and Evolution.
sources.note: Star formation and collapse toward hydrostatic support.

claims.notes: This is the structural backbone of the stellar lane.

claims.statement: The stellar thermal-equilibrium anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when energy generation and transport remain in quasi-steady balance over the relevant stellar timescale.

constraints: energy generation and transport must both be represented
thermal balance is coupled to hydrostatic balance but not identical to it
the lane should not claim exact steady state for every epoch

sources.citation: ESA Science & Technology, Stellar Processes and Evolution.
sources.note: Quasi-steady stellar evolution with energy flow.

sources.citation: NASA Technical Reports Server, Stellar evolution: A survey with analytic.

claims.notes: Thermal balance is the transport/energy side of the stellar lane.

claims.statement: The stellar equation-of-state anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when pressure is treated as a function of density, temperature, and composition in stellar plasma.

constraints: the EOS must be plasma-based rather than planetary-rubble-based
composition dependence must remain explicit
degeneracy support or ideal-gas limits may appear depending on regime

claims.notes: The EOS is the support law used by the star lane.

claims.statement: The stellar opacity anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when radiative transport resistance is treated as a composition-dependent material property that feeds back into structure.

constraints: opacity must affect radiative transport, not hydrostatic force balance directly
composition changes are allowed to alter opacity
the lane should not collapse radiative and convective transport into one undifferentiated term

claims.notes: Opacity is one of the main stellar transport controls.

claims.statement: The nuclear-reaction-rate anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when the lane treats fusion and capture rates as temperature-, density-, and composition-dependent microphysics inputs.

constraints: reaction rates must be inside the stellar interior, not the planetary lane
rate dependence on stellar conditions must stay explicit
reaction rates feed burning stages and nucleosynthesis

sources.citation: NASA/CP-2006-21454, Nucleosynthesis: Stellar and Solar Abundances and Atomic Data.

claims.statement: The hydrogen-burning anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when it is treated as the main hydrogen-fusion stage of stellar evolution.

constraints: hydrogen burning must precede later fusion stages in the lane order
the claim must remain about stellar fusion, not planetary chemistry
the stage may be proton-proton chain or CNO dominated depending on stellar mass

sources.citation: NASA Cosmicopia, Nucleosynthesis.

claims.notes: Hydrogen burning is the primary early stellar power source.

claims.statement: The helium-burning anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when it is treated as the post-main-sequence fusion stage that produces heavier nuclei from helium fuel.

constraints: helium burning must follow hydrogen burning in the stage ladder
the claim must remain about stellar interior burning
heavier-nucleus production must remain explicit

sources.citation: NASA/CP-2006-21454, Nucleosynthesis: Stellar and Solar Abundances and Atomic Data.

claims.notes: Helium burning is the next composition-changing stage.

claims.statement: The advanced-burning-stages anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when the lane includes the late fusion ladder that produces progressively heavier nuclei before final collapse or dispersal.

constraints: advanced burning must stay later than helium burning
the stage ladder should remain distinct from the generic nucleosynthesis umbrella
the lane should not assert a universal final product for every stellar mass

claims.statement: The stellar-nucleosynthesis anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when the lane treats stars as element-producing engines whose burning stages and reaction networks alter the abundance pattern of the matter they eject.

constraints: nucleosynthesis must be inside stellar evolution
the claim must not collapse into prebiotic chemistry or biology
element creation should remain stage-ordered and composition-aware

feedback should affect opacity and EOS explicitly
the lane may bridge onward to chemistry but should not claim that bridge as a finished biology result

claims.statement: The stellar-oscillation anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when the lane treats standing acoustic and gravity modes as observable stellar variability rather than as a consciousness mechanism.

the mode language should not be upgraded to consciousness

the lane should not become a consciousness bridge

claims.statement: The solar-dynamo anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when convection and rotation are treated as the source of large-scale magnetic-field generation in the Sun.

constraints: the dynamo must remain a magnetic-field-generation mechanism
the lane should not collapse into flare statistics alone
rotation-convection coupling must remain explicit

constraints: cycle modulation must remain a magnetic-activity observable

the cycle is downstream of dynamo action
